items.title: Jalen Brunson last night:
items.author: Justin Kubatko
items.body_excerpt:
Jalen Brunson last night:

✅ 45 PTS
✅ 14-27 FG
✅ 4-7 3P
✅ 13-15 FT

Brunson is the fourth player to score at least 45 points in a Finals-clinching win.

He scored 47.9% of New York's points, the fifth-highest such figure in a Finals game.

Read, share, subscribe ⬇️

images.alt:
Prediction: Knicks in 6
To win a series without home-court advantage, you’ve got to get a game on the road. The Knicks have done that in eight of the 10 playoff series they’ve played in the Brunson era, including the last six. They enter this matchup with a significant rest advantage, with all five Spurs starters having played more minutes in these playoffs than any member of the Knicks. They won’t have the best player in the series; they’re also deeper in offensive talent than the Thunder team that just took the Spurs the distance without two of its three best creators.

Wembanyama is a wholly different challenge than anything the Knicks have yet to face. But the Knicks have spent the last month proving themselves capable of handling lots of different kinds of challenges, showcasing the kind of dynamism and versatility on both ends of the floor that makes them built to solve even the most complex problems.

I think they get one in San Antonio. I think they find just enough answers. And I think the result is … well, something that’s seemed unthinkable for most of the last 53 years.

items.title: I remember asking Knicks asst coach Rick Brunson why his son Jalen handles the NY media spotlight with so much ease and aplomb.
items.author: Mark Jones
items.body_excerpt:
I remember asking Knicks asst coach Rick Brunson why his son Jalen handles the NY media spotlight with so much ease and aplomb. 

Rick told me
 “when Jalen first signed in NY I sat down with him and made him watch the Netflix doc on Derek Jeter. Watch how Jeter deals the media smoke”

Jalen is 🥶 💯

items.title: The Knicks are NBA Champions for the first time in 53 years 🏆
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
The Knicks are NBA Champions for the first time in 53 years 🏆

Jalen Brunson (45 PTS) and NYK win Game 5 on the road, defeat San Antonio 4-1 in the NBA Finals, and capture their third championship in franchise history!

Wins in San Antonio during NBA Finals + conference finals:

items.title: For the first time since 1973, the Knicks are NBA CHAMPIONS 🏆
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
For the first time since 1973, the Knicks are NBA CHAMPIONS 🏆

The 53-year gap between championships is the longest in NBA history!

The wait is over, New York Knicks 👏

items.title: Karl-Anthony Towns on Wemby:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Karl-Anthony Towns on Wemby: 

"He's going to be something special for a lot of years to come.  Once-in-a-generation athlete.

"Shout out to the Spurs, too.  They're the real deal.

"Shout out to Jersey's own, Dylan Harper; he's the real deal."

items.title: Jalen Brunson on the Spurs: “That's a hell of a group. That's a well-coached (team). The culture they have is clearly evident. They have it. And so a lot of respect to them and what they brought to the table. I'm just happy were able to find ways to win four games."
items.author: Tom Orsborn
